description: Pi coding agent (pi.dev) - minimal terminal harness with TypeScript extensibility

agent_file.note: All matching files are concatenated at startup. Walks up directory tree from cwd. Also accepts CLAUDE.md as alternative filename.

rules.native_name: No dedicated rules directory — embedded in AGENTS.md
rules.note: Pi has no separate rules directory. All rules content must live in AGENTS.md files.

skills.note: Follows agentskills.io standard. Invoke via /skill:name or agent auto-loads. Can be loaded from pi packages.

commands.native_name: Slash commands via TypeScript extensions only
commands.note: Commands are NOT a separate file primitive. Custom commands must be registered in TypeScript extensions. Built-in commands (/model, /reload, etc.) are part of pi core.

custom_tools.note: Tools defined in extensions via SDK. Built-in tools: read, write, edit, bash, grep, find, ls. No separate tools directory.

hooks.native_name: Event hooks via TypeScript extensions only
hooks.note: Hooks are event listeners in TypeScript extensions, not shell scripts. No standalone hooks config.

mcp_servers.note: Pi does not have built-in MCP server support. MCP integration requires building a TypeScript extension that implements MCP client protocol. Workaround: use executor gateway pattern if MCP tools are needed.

subagents.note: Pi philosophy: 'Ask pi to build what you want' or use third-party pi packages. Sub-agent delegation is not a built-in feature. Workaround: build via extensions or use prompt templates/skills.

plugins.note: Pi calls them 'extensions' not plugins. TypeScript/JS modules loaded at startup. Can define tools, commands, hooks, UI widgets, custom providers. Managed via 'pi install/remove/update' CLI.

prompt_templates.native_name: prompts/ directory (Markdown files with optional Mustache variables)
prompt_templates.note: Markdown files with optional {{var}} syntax. Invoke via /name in editor to expand inline. Can be packaged and distributed via pi packages.
